items.title: Labor unrest at Samsung may worsen memory chip supply issues
items.description: Tens of thousands of Samsung Electronics workers held a rally at the company’s Pyeongtaek campus in South Korea on Thursday to signal they are prepared to walk off the job for an 18-day strike next month.

items.title: Tesla drops Musk’s $29B ‘interim’ award after Delaware court restored larger pay package
items.description: The company is following through on its promise to not let Musk "double dip" if he won his appeal to restore Tesla's 2018 compensation package, worth $56 billion.

items.description: WhatsApp is rolling out prepaid phone recharges to its 500 million users in India via PayU.

items.description: Topics can range from "School," "Entertainment," and "Lifestyle" to "Travel," "Writing," and "Health and Wellbeing," among others, Meta says.

items.title: France confirms data breach at government agency that manages citizens’ IDs
items.description: The French government agency that issues and manages national IDs, passports, and other documents announced that hackers stole the personal information of an unspecified number of citizens.
